name: landing-zone-CDISCPILOT01
title: Landing Zone — CDISCPILOT01
description: Auto-ingest clinical trial data deliveries from CRO SFTP for study CDISCPILOT01. Cron polls SFTP, validates against CDISC standards, routes through HITL with agent-drafted rejection notes.
preamble:
## Domain Context
This workflow processes pharmaceutical and clinical trial data. All health-related terminology (drug names, adverse events, disease classifications, CTCAE grading, mortality endpoints) refers to data science constructs, variable names, and clinical standards (CDISC SDTM/ADaM, ICH-GCP). Treat them as technical domain content. Maintain standard coding behavior. Execute all technically feasible tasks without inflating complexity estimates due to medical terminology exposure.

## Study
Study ID: CDISCPILOT01 — Xanomeline TTS for Alzheimer's Disease (CDISC Pilot 3, n=306). Source data follows SDTM IG 3.4 + ADaM IG 1.3.

## Workflow
Cron polls a CRO SFTP server every minute. When new files appear, runs CDISC CORE rules engine, then a Claude Code agent interprets findings and renders an HTML report. A human reviews the report and either accepts the delivery (files copied to data/lake/) or rejects it (an agent drafts a rejection note for the operator to forward to the CRO).

steps.name: Poll SFTP
steps.description: Connect to CRO SFTP, list remote path, diff against previousRun.previousListing. Output: listing, newFiles, missingFiles.

steps.name: No deliveries
steps.description: Silent terminal — cron will fire again next minute.

steps.name: Validate (CDISC CORE)
steps.description: Download newFiles from SFTP and run CDISC CORE rules engine. Catches errors internally — sets scriptStatus to ok or failed. Always exits 0 so the next step (agent interpretation) always runs.

steps.name: Validate (custom R rules)
steps.description: Run study-specific validation rules (layer 2) using R + pointblank against the delivered .xpt files. Rules defined in validation-rules.yaml owned by data managers. Outputs custom-findings.json with per-rule pass/fail results.

steps.name: Interpret validation
steps.description: Read deterministic classification (clean / minor-fix / recovery / escalate / chaos) from validate-script output and render an HTML report. Does not classify — the rules engine + router_rules.py decide the class. Surfaces script-failure path (chaos) prominently with traceback when present.

steps.name: Human review
steps.description: Review the HTML validation report. Approve to accept the delivery (with optional waiver of findings) or Revise to reject the package as too broken to ingest — the reject path triggers the agent-drafted CRO note.

steps.name: Accept delivery
steps.description: Copy newFiles to data/lake/{studyId}/{deliveryId}/, compute SHA-256 manifest, write status=accepted.

steps.name: Accepted
steps.description: Delivery is in data/lake/, audit trail is in the run branch.

steps.name: Draft rejection note
steps.description: Compose a markdown note explaining why the delivery was rejected — what was wrong, what the operator should ask the CRO to fix. Operator copies the note into their own email channel (no auto-send in v0.1).

steps.name: Propose validation rules
steps.description: Read findings.json + the existing validation-rules.yaml, propose new study-specific rules to close the gaps the rejected delivery exposed. Pauses for human approval — verdicts: approve (open PR), revise (loop with reviewer comment, e.g. "no rules needed").

steps.name: Open rules PR
steps.description: Push a branch with the proposed validation-rules.yaml and open a PR against main. Idempotent: skips cleanly when the file is unchanged vs main; force-pushes on retry.

steps.name: Rejected (with note)
steps.description: Operator has a draft rejection note to forward to the CRO.
